deathPlace.address: Battle of Towton Field, Towton, England

person.userCertifiedLifeSummary:
«b»Parents«/b»
Richard Scrope, Esq. was born circa 1442 at Bentley & Upsdale, Yorkshire, Langley Castle, Durham, England, the son of Sir Henry le Scrope and Elizabeth le Scrope.

«b»Marriage & Children«/b»
He and Eleanor Washbourne obtained a marriage license on 27 November 1467 at Sheriff Hutton, Yorkshire, England. 

«b»They had 1 son & 8 daughters:«/b»
1.) Stephen

2.) Elizabeth, wife of Sir William, 2nd Viscount Beaumont, & of Sir John de Vere, 13th Earl of Oxford

3.) Margaret, wife of Sir Edmund de la Pole, 3rd Duke of Suffolk

4.) Eleanor, wife of Sir Thomas Wyndham

5.) Anne, a nun at Barking

6.) Mary, wife of Edward Jernegan, & of Sir William Kingston

7.) Katherine, wife of Richard Audley, Esq

8.) Joan (Jane), wife of Thomas Brewes

9.) Dorothy

«b»Death«/b»
Richard Scrope, Esq. left a will on 4 April. His estate was probated on 28 June 1485.

zh.lifeSummary: 當Sir Richard le Scrope於1442年出生在英國越可爾卡素 博爾頓時，他的父親Henry Scrope24歲，母親Elizabeth Scrope23歲。他於1467年11月26日與Lady Alianor de Washbourne在英國英格蘭越可爾黑利福 赫頓結婚。他們至少是3個女兒的父母。他去世於1485年4月4日在英國英格蘭諾斯 越可爾堂頓享年43歲，埋葬在英國倫敦布拉可福利亞斯。

nameMeanings.name: le
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Vietnamese Thanh, Hung, Minh, Dung, Tuan, Hai, Hoa, Thuy, Son, Anh, Long, Hoang.  Chinese Hong, Sang, Chi, Dong, Tong, Lai, Hang, Man, Han, Ho, Phoung, Chan.  Korean Tam, Phong, Nam, Thai, Tuong, Chung, Thach, Tinh, Manh, Tham, Uyen, Vang.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 樂, meaning ‘happiness’ in Chinese: from the first element of the personal name Le Fu (樂父), style name of Yan, son of the Duke Dai of Song (ruler of the state of Song, reigned 799–766  BC ). Yan's grandson Le Ju (樂莒) inherited Le (樂) as his surname.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 勒: (i) from the second element of the placename Shu-Le (疏勒), an ancient state (located in present-day Xinjiang Uyghur autonomous region) during the Western Han dynasty (206  BC –25  AD ). (ii) a surname near the Bohai Sea region during the Han dynasty (206  BC –220  AD ). (iii) a surname traced back to the Hun ethnic group in northern China during the Jin dynasty (265–420  AD ).

variants.name: Yue
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese: Chi, Feng, Gang, Wei, Chun, Guo, Jin, Kin, Ping, Yan, Yun, Zhang, Chau, Du, Hau, Hung, My, Sau.

Mandarin form of the surname 岳 (or 嶽), meaning ‘high mountain’ in Chinese: traced back to Si Yue (四岳 or 四嶽), said to be the title of an official post in charge of sacrificial rituals on the site of famous mountains during the reign of the legendary Emperor Yao (c. 24th century BC ) and Emperor Shun (c. 23rd century BC ).

variants.name: Lok
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Ping, Tat, Wing, Ying Lin, Chi Ming, Ching Wah, Chun, Chung Man, Han, Kam, Kam Tim, Kin.
Chinese: Cantonese form of the surname 樂, see   Le   1.
Chinese: Cantonese form of the surnames 陸, 鹿, 逯 and 禄, see   Lu   5 and (7-9).

variants.name: Li
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Wei, Ming, Li, Jian, Hong, Yan, Ping, Jing, Yong, Ying, Hui, Jin, Min, Chung.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 李, meaning ‘plum’: (i) said to have been adopted in place of the Chinese surname 理 (also pronounced Li; meaning ‘rules’ in Chinese) by Li Zhen (利貞), son of an official called Li Zheng (理徵) in the late Shang dynasty (1600–1046  BC ). It is said that Li Zhen's father incurred the displeasure of King Zhou of Shang (died 1046  BC ) and Li Zhen fled to a wasteland area, where he survived on wild plums. After his return to civilization, he changed his surname to Li (李 ‘plum’), a word with the same pronunciation. The original surname, 理, is said to be from Li Guan (理官), the name of a post of officials in charge of legislation in ancient China. Li Er (李耳), also known as Lao Tzu or Lao Zi, traditionally regarded as the founder of Taoism, is believed to have been a descendant of Li Zhen. (ii) adopted as a surname by the Chi Li (叱李) family from the Xianbei ethnic group during the Northern Wei dynasty (386–534  AD ). (iii) in the early years of the Tang dynasty (618–907  AD ), sixteen high officials with different surnames were granted the surname Li (李), the surname of the royal family of the Tang dynasty, by Emperor Taizong of Tang (died 649  BC ), because of their contributions to the establishment of the Tang regime. (iv) the surname can also be traced back to several minority ethnic groups in ancient China.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 黎, meaning ‘black’ or ‘multitude’ in ancient Chinese: (i) from Li (黎), the name of an ancient state (located in Changzhi in Shanxi province) during the Shang dynasty (1600–1046  BC ), which was annexed in the Western Zhou dynasty (1046–771  BC ) and later granted to Fengshu, also known as Li Hou (黎侯, 'Marquis Li'). (ii) adopted as a surname by the Su Li (素黎) family, members of the Xianbei ethnic group, during the Northern Wei dynasty (386–534  AD ).

nameMeanings.name: Scrope
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of Family Names in Britain and Ireland © University of the West of England 2016
meanings: Norman: of uncertain origin. Tengvik suggests derivation from the Old Scandinavian personal name Skrópi (of doubtful etymology, but compare Old Scandinavian skrópa-maðr ‘hypocrite’, containing the genitive plural form of skrópar ‘sham, hypocrisy’). It is possible, but not certain, that this personal name forms the first element of the Notts place-name Scrooby. Otherwise there is no evidence for its currency in England or, more relevantly, in Normandy. Richards Castle (in Herefs and Shrops) was apparently named after Richard Scrope, who held estates in Herefs and Worcs in 1066 and was a Norman favourite of Edward the Confessor. The Scrope family, whose name is traditionally pronounced /skru:p/, was politically important in medieval England, and acquired estates in Lincs (Barton on Humber), NR Yorks (Castle Bolton), and in ER Yorks (Flotmanby). Richard (le) Scrope of Bolton, in Wensleydale, was Lord Chancellor in the reign of Richard II. The use of le and de in the surname, suggesting either a nickname or a locative name, must be treated with caution because these were often added gratuitously to Anglo-Norman surnames as a sign of high social status.

nameMeanings.name: Richard
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: One of the most enduringly successful of the Old French personal names introduced into Britain by the Normans. It is of Germanic (Frankish) origin, derived from rīc ‘power’ + hard ‘strong, hardy’. It has enjoyed continuous popularity in England from the Conquest to the present day, influenced by the fact that it was borne by three kings of England, in particular Richard I ( 1157–99 ). He was king for only ten years ( 1189–99 ), most of which he spent in warfare abroad, taking part in the Third Crusade and costing the people of England considerable sums in taxes. Nevertheless, he achieved the status of a folk hero, and was never in England long enough to disappoint popular faith in his goodness and justice. He was also Duke of Aquitaine and Normandy and Count of Anjou, fiefs which he held at a time of maximum English expansion in France. His exploits as a leader of the Third Crusade earned him the nickname ‘Coeur de Lion’ or ‘Lionheart’ and a permanent place in popular imagination, in which he was even more firmly enshrined by Sir Walter Scott's novel Ivanhoe ( 1820 ).

variants.name: Rikert

It has enjoyed continuous popularity in England from the Conquest to the present day, influenced by the fact that it was borne by three kings of England, in particular Richard I ( 1157–99 ). He was king for only ten years ( 1189–99 ), most of which he spent in warfare abroad, taking part in the Third Crusade and costing the people of England considerable sums in taxes. His exploits as a leader of the Third Crusade earned him the nickname ‘Coeur de Lion’ or ‘Lionheart’ and a permanent place in popular imagination, in which he was even more firmly enshrined by Sir Walter Scott's novel Ivanhoe ( 1820 ).

historicEvents.title: Wars of the Roses
historicEvents.description: Wars of the Roses was a series of English civil wars for control of the throne of England between the royal families House of Lancaster and the House of York. The name of the war was derived as the red rose was the symbol of the Lancastrians and the white rose was the symbol of the Yorks.

en.lifeSummary: When Sir Richard le Scrope was born in 1442, in Castle Bolton, Yorkshire, England, his father, Henry Scrope, was 24 and his mother, Elizabeth Scrope, was 23. He married Lady Alianor de Washbourne on 26 November 1467, in Sheriff Hutton, Yorkshire, England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 3 daughters. He died on 4 April 1485, in Towton, North Yorkshire, England, United Kingdom, at the age of 43, and was buried in Blackfriars, London, England.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Sir Richard le Scrope est né en 1442, à Castle Bolton, Everwyck, Angleterre, son père, Henry Scrope, avait 24 ans et sa mère, Elizabeth Scrope, avait 23 ans. Il a épousé Lady Alianor de Washbourne le 26 novembre 1467, à Sheriff Hutton, Everwyck, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 3 filles. Il est décédé le 4 avril 1485, à Towton, North Yorkshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, à l’âge de 43 ans, et a été enterré à Blackfriars, Londres, Angleterre.

ja.lifeSummary: Sir Richard le Scropeが1442年にイギリスヨークシュアイアカストル ボルトンで生まれた時，父Henry Scropeは24歳，母Elizabeth Scropeは23歳であった。1467年11月26日にイギリスイギリスヨークシュアイアシェリッフ ハットオンでLady Alianor de Washbourneと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも3人の娘の親であった。彼は1485年4月4日にイギリスイギリスノース ヨークシュアイアトウトンで43歳で死去し，イギリスロンドンブラクフリアルズにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Sir Richard le Scrope(이)가 1442년 영국 요르크실르 카스틀 볼턴에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Henry Scrope(은)는 24세였고, 그의 어머니 Elizabeth Scrope(은)는 23세였다. 그는 1467년 11월 26일 영국 잉글랜드 요르크실르 셔이프 후턴에서 Lady Alianor de Washbourne(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 세 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1485년 4월 4일 영국 잉글랜드 노르스 요르크실르 타우턴에서 43세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 런던 블랙프리아르스에 묻혔다.

Ele casou-se com Lady Alianor de Washbourne em 26 de novembro de 1467, em Sheriff Hutton, Yorkshire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom. Eles tiveram pelo menos 3 filhas. ru.lifeSummary: Когда Sir Richard le Scrope родился в 1442 г. в местности кастл болтон, еркшир, Англия, его отцу по имени Henry Scrope было 24 года, а матери, которую звали Elizabeth Scrope, 23. Он женился на женщине по имени Lady Alianor de Washbourne 26 ноября 1467 г. в местности ширифф хуттон, еркшир, Англия, Великобритания. У них было по крайней мере 3 дочери. Он умер 4 апреля 1485 г. в местности тоутон, норт еркшир, Англия, Великобритания в возрасте 43 лет и был похоронен в местности блэккфрярс, Лондон, Англия.

Se casó con Lady Alianor de Washbourne el 26 de noviembre de 1467, en Sheriff Hutton, Yorkshire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido. Fueron padres de por lo menos 3 hijas. Murió el 4 de abril de 1485, en Towton, North Yorkshire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido, a la edad de 43 años, y fue sepultado en Blackfriars, London, Inglaterra.
